rationale: Nigeria's 460MW total demand by 2030 has no AWS region — the largest economy in Africa without an AWS availability zone. 200M+ population and growing fintech sector justify standalone region.
trigger: Local data localisation enforcement + anchor tenant pre-commitments

rationale: Kenya's 72MW total demand and geothermal RE advantage make it the most viable East Africa AWS region. Oracle Cloud announced its first Kenya region (iXAfrica) in 2025.
trigger: Oracle Cloud Kenya launch creates competitive pressure; Microsoft/G42 campus precedent

rationale: Morocco's 177MW demand, data residency law, and Naver 500MW campus signal that all three hyperscalers will follow. Microsoft already has Azure regions in South Africa.
trigger: Naver/NVIDIA campus Phase 1 commissioning; Morocco data residency enforcement

rationale: Egypt's 430MW demand and 19 subsea cable connections make it a natural Google Cloud gateway for North Africa and the Nile corridor.
trigger: Huawei Cloud commissioning creates competitive urgency; GITEX Africa 2026 announcements expected

rationale: Google Cloud has one SA region. SA's $17.4B cloud market by 2030 justifies a second availability zone and potential expansion to Durban.
trigger: AWS and Azure expanding; Google cannot cede SA enterprise market

operator: Alibaba Cloud / Huawei Cloud ECOWAS hub
rationale: Chinese cloud providers are rapidly expanding into Francophone West Africa. Dakar is strategically positioned as a 2Africa cable landing station with political alignment.
trigger: AfCFTA digital trade provisions; Chinese DFI infrastructure lending

rationale: Equinix's IBX model requires anchor tenants and carrier density. Morocco now has both. Following pattern of Equinix JHB entry via MainOne/CIV acquisition.
trigger: Naver campus; N+ONE acquisition or JV; Morocco IXP maturity

rationale: Teraco's parent Digital Realty has global mandate to follow hyperscaler footprint. Nigeria is the logical next market after South Africa given AWS/Google Cloud entry.
trigger: AWS or Google Cloud Nigeria launch; anchor tenant pre-leases

rationale: Algeria's 133MW demand, very low electricity tariff ($0.04/kWh), and near-zero existing neutral colocation makes it one of Africa's largest greenfield opportunities.
trigger: Algeria investment liberalisation; North Africa digital integration; 2Africa cable

rationale: Ethiopia's near-100% RE grid, world-class altitude cooling, and 39MW total demand justify a second neutral facility.
trigger: Ethio Telecom market liberalisation; IFC/BII development finance

rationale: MTN Nigeria's 76M subscribers and national backhaul network justify DC assets in Abuja (federal government workloads) and Kano (northern Nigeria demand centre).
trigger: MTN Group data centre strategy announcement; Nigerian government cloud migration

rationale: Mombasa is Kenya's second city and a critical port logistics hub. TEAMS/EASSY cable landing station makes it a natural edge node.
trigger: 5G rollout in coastal Kenya; port automation digitisation

operator: Orange Africa / MTN West Africa shared facility
rationale: Orange Africa and MTN both operate in West Africa. A shared telco-neutral DC in Accra or Abidjan would serve both operators.
trigger: West African telecom regulator cross-border data framework

rationale: Tanzania's 47MW demand and SEACOM cable landing position justifies Vodacom building a carrier-neutral facility.
trigger: Tanzania National ICT Strategy 2025–2030; 4G/5G rollout

rationale: Nigeria's three non-Lagos cities collectively represent 60M+ people. Edge DCs reduce Lagos dependency for government services, health AI, and e-commerce.
trigger: Nigeria government decentralisation policy; e-health and e-education mandates

rationale: Egypt's 430MW demand is 90%+ Cairo-concentrated. Alexandria (4M+ population) and Aswan (Nile Valley hub) are natural edge nodes.
trigger: Egypt Vision 2030 digital decentralisation; National Data Cloud Policy

rationale: Kenya's Vision 2030 digital agenda requires regional digital services. Kisumu, Nakuru and Eldoret serve 15M+ Kenyans outside Nairobi.
trigger: Kenyan government county-level e-services rollout; 5G secondary city expansion

rationale: Ghana's 48MW demand supports a national government cloud DC. Ghana's data protection law is among West Africa's most advanced.
trigger: Ghana e-government strategy 2025; World Bank Digital Ghana project

rationale: Algeria meets MVS at 133MW total demand. Government has strategic interest in reducing dependence on European cloud. Very low electricity tariff.
trigger: Algerian Digital Economy Act; North Africa sovereign compute initiative

rationale: Tanzania's 47MW demand and Vision 2025 digital agenda justify a national sovereign data facility.
trigger: Tanzania e-government portal; digital ID national rollout

rationale: Nigeria's fintech processing 200M+ daily transactions requires sovereign, low-latency infrastructure.
trigger: CBN data localisation enforcement; open banking API framework

operator: Equity Group / M-Pesa / I&M Bank consortium
rationale: Kenya's digital banking ecosystem drives real-time AI fraud detection and credit scoring.
trigger: CBK AI risk management framework; Kenya open banking rollout

operator: Standard Bank / Absa / Nedbank AI DC
rationale: South Africa's Big 4 banks are deploying AI for fraud, credit, and AML. A dedicated AI infrastructure facility is emerging.
trigger: FSCA AI governance framework; Basel IV compute requirements
